_comment: Measured 2026-05-26 on 100-doc eval_v3 with real claude binary ingest. Tracks 3 no-training improvements over v0.1.0 baseline.

plus_token_overlap._change: + score_entry now does single-token presence (0.7) + Jaccard for multi-token

plus_tuned_weights_HEURISTIC._key_insight: lambda_cons=0 removes flat-mass baseline that was diluting softmax

plus_tuned_weights_LLM._note: tuned weights make LLM planner slightly worse than heuristic — sharper softmax penalizes LLM's broader expansions

v0.1.0_to_final_heuristic.cost: ZERO — no training, no LLM per query, no model swap

regression_thresholds_default._comment: Floor for the current tuned-default + heuristic-planner path. Below = something broke.

interpretation: Three no-training changes (LLM-plan optional, token-overlap, tuned weights) lifted Recall@3 from 0.30 to 0.875 — a 2.9x improvement.
The biggest single lever was AddressingWeights tuning: lambda_cons=0 + lower T0 (sharper softmax). The default constant-bias was wasting most of the prior signal.
Token-overlap relaxation lets multi-word entity names match partial expansions ('Linus' -> 'Linus Torvalds' at 0.7).
LlmQueryPlanner is still valuable for tier-5 multi-hop (only path that scores > 0.2 there), but no longer dominant for tier-1/2/3.
v0.1.0 numbers preserved here as a reference point — DO NOT regress below regression_thresholds_default.
